I # Alex's Taste Profile
*Auto-generated from 70 past gigs. Last updated: 2026-03-24*

## What Makes a Dream Gig

The pattern is clear across every top-rated venue: **educated, wealthy, cultured audience** is the #1 factor. The venue quality, pay, and tips are secondary — the audience makes or breaks it. Alex's best gigs are where smart, receptive people appreciate classical and Latin guitar.

### Dream Gig Traits (8.5+ overall)
- Educated, wealthy, cultured audience that genuinely appreciates music
- Historic or architecturally beautiful setting
- Intimate atmosphere (not a big noisy room)
- Rebooked regularly (not one-offs)
- Great pay + great tips
- French restaurants, upscale inns, private clubs, mountain wineries

### Dream Venues (for reference)
- **University Club DC** (9.5) — "Best audience I've ever played for — extremely intelligent, cultured"
- **The Inn at Perry Cabin** (9.5) — "Very upscale hotel. Amazing clientele"
- **L'Auberge Chez Francois** (9.3) — "Perfect match for classical guitar. Educated wealthy audience"
- **Big Cork Vineyards** (9.3) — "Beautiful winery in the mountains, great architecture"
- **Good Spirit Farm Winery** (9.0) — "Smart, engaged people. Beautiful mountain winery"
- **Belle Haven Country Club** (9.0) — "Rich upscale audience. Beautiful historic setting"
- **Army Navy Country Club** (8.0) — "DREAM GIG. Great smart people, wine dinners, 8 years"
- **Bistrot Lepic** (7.8 but rated 9/10) — "AMAZING audience, super smart, rich, cultured. Dark french restaurant. FIND MORE LIKE THIS"
- **Conrad Washington DC** (7.0 but rated 9/10) — "AMAZING venue, fit in so well. FIND MORE LIKE THIS"
- **The Milton Inn** (6.5 but rated 9/10) — "GOLD STANDARD. Beautiful and historic. Classy smart upscale people"

## Venue Categories Ranked

### Tier 1 — Actively Hunt For These
1. **Upscale French/European restaurants** — Best audience match. Educated, cultured crowd. Dark intimate settings. (L'Auberge, Bistrot Lepic, Elkridge Furnace Inn)
2. **Historic private clubs** — Prestigious, intelligent members. No tips but great pay. (University Club, Maryland Club, Cosmos Club)
3. **Upscale country clubs with wine dinners** — Rich educated crowd, recurring bookings. (Army Navy, Belle Haven, River Bend)
4. **Luxury boutique hotels** — Upscale travelers, great ambiance. Restaurant/lounge setting preferred. (Inn at Perry Cabin, Conrad DC)
5. **Mountain/architectural wineries** — Beautiful settings attract better crowds. (Big Cork, Good Spirit Farm, Windridge, Black Ankle)

### Tier 2 — Good Calendar Fillers
6. **Nice restaurants in upscale areas** — Alexandria VA, Reston, St Michaels, Bethesda. Area matters as much as venue. (Royal Restaurant, Great Sage, Woodberry Kitchen)
7. **Wine bars in wealthy neighborhoods** — Small, intimate, educated crowd. (Lake Anne, Simple Theory)
8. **Eastern Shore venues** — St Michaels especially. Very upscale and classy people during tourist season.
9. **Average wineries** — Reliable pay, decent crowd. Not exciting but fill the calendar. (50 West, Sunset Hills, Xella)

### Tier 3 — Lower Priority
10. **Sports-focused country clubs** — Wrong crowd for classical guitar but still worth a shot. (1757 Golf Club, Holly Hills, Chartwell)
11. **Generic hotels** — Meh audience, no tips. Still email them.
12. **Arlington VA venues** — Hit or miss despite being wealthy. Lower expectations.
13. **Malls** — Only worth it for holiday pay.

### Tier 4 — Skip (Don't Waste the Email)
14. **Venues with terrible owners/people** — Creek's Edge, Janemark, Le Méridien
15. **Sports bars / loud nightlife** — Classical guitar literally doesn't work here
16. **Chain restaurants/hotels** — No decision-maker to reach, corporate booking

## Audience Red Flags
- "Snotty for no reason" (Chartwell) — upper middle class ≠ cultured
- "Sports bar vibes" — wrong crowd entirely
- "Redneck" anything — will work for money but not fulfilling
- Winter Eastern Shore — "nightmare full of locals"
- Arlington VA — surprisingly bad despite being wealthy

## Audience Green Flags
- "Educated wealthy cultured" — the holy trinity
- "Smart, engaged, receptive" — they actually listen
- "Wine dinners" — perfect setting for classical guitar
- French/European dining — naturally matches the music style
- "Historic" anything — attracts a better class of patron
- Museum events, private events in upscale venues

## Location Sweet Spots — Wealthy Areas by State

### Washington DC
- Georgetown, Dupont Circle, Kalorama, Embassy Row
- Cleveland Park, Woodley Park, Spring Valley
- Capitol Hill (nicer blocks), Logan Circle

### Maryland — DC Suburbs (Tier 1)
- Potomac ($212k median income), Chevy Chase, Bethesda
- Martin's Additions ($553k avg income), Travilah
- Rockville (nice parts), Cabin John, Glen Echo

### Maryland — Baltimore Area
- Roland Park, Guilford, Homeland, Mt. Washington
- Ruxton, Lutherville, Towson (nicer areas)
- Ellicott City, Clarksville, Columbia (Hickory Ridge)

### Maryland — Eastern Shore
- St. Michaels, Easton (6th richest small town in US), Oxford
- Tilghman Island (summer/tourist season only)
- Kent Island, Centreville, Chestertown
- *Note: winter Eastern Shore = avoid (locals only, wrong crowd)*

### Maryland — Annapolis Area
- Annapolis (downtown, Eastport, Naval Academy area)
- Severna Park, Arnold, Edgewater
- Crofton, Gambrills

### Virginia — Northern Virginia (Tier 1)
- Great Falls ($353k mean income, $2.4M median home)
- McLean ($200k median income), Tysons Corner
- Alexandria Old Town, Del Ray
- Reston, Herndon (nicer parts), Vienna

### Virginia — Loudoun County
- Middleburg, Leesburg, Purcellville
- Ashburn (newer money), South Riding
- *Great for winery circuit*

### Virginia — Other
- Charlottesville (university town, cultured)
- Shenandoah Valley wine country
- *Avoid: Arlington (hit or miss despite wealth)*

### Delaware
- Greenville ($241k avg income — wealthiest in DE)
- Hockessin, Centreville, Montchanin
- Wilmington (Chateau Country, Brandywine Valley)
- Rehoboth Beach, Lewes (summer crowd)

### Pennsylvania (within 2-hour radius)
- Main Line: Gladwyne, Bryn Mawr, Villanova, Haverford, Devon ($221k mean income)
- Chester County: West Pikeland ($262k mean income), Kennett Square, Unionville
- Brandywine Valley, Chadds Ford
- Lancaster County: upscale farm-to-table scene

### West Virginia (edge of radius)
- Shepherdstown (college town)
- Charles Town, Harpers Ferry area
- *Limited but some winery/inn opportunities*

## What to Search For (Pipeline Targets — Ranked by Priority)
1. **Luxury hotels** — Ritz-Carlton, Four Seasons, Conrad, Waldorf, Salamander, boutique hotels with restaurants/lounges
2. **Historic fine dining** — Milton Inn-type gems, old inns with character, chef-driven restaurants in historic buildings
3. **French/European bistros** — intimate, dark, romantic, educated clientele (Bistrot Lepic was 9/10)
4. **Elite private clubs** — university clubs, city clubs, yacht clubs, Cosmos Club-type venues in every major city
5. **Wine bars** — in wealthy neighborhoods, educated crowd, NOT sports bars that serve wine
6. **Upscale country clubs with wine dinners** — Belle Haven, Army Navy, River Bend style
7. **Mountain wineries with great architecture** — Big Cork, Good Spirit Farm style, beautiful settings
8. **Corporate events / law firms** — DC/Bethesda/Tysons corridor, holiday parties, firm retreats
9. **Upscale Latin/international restaurants** — Alma Cocina Latina type, cultured crowd
10. **Italian fine dining** — not casual Italian, white tablecloth
11. **Museum event spaces** — maritime museums, art galleries, cultural centers
12. **Upscale restaurants in wealthy areas** — Georgetown, Alexandria, Bethesda, Reston, Main Line PA
13. **Historic inns and B&Bs** — Eastern Shore, Shenandoah Valley, Brandywine Valley

**DO NOT use "live music" as a search keyword** — search by venue type and quality. Venues don't need to already have live music.

## Anti-Targets (Skip These)
- Sports bars, sports-focused clubs
- Chain restaurants/hotels
- Casual/fast-casual dining
- Venues in strip malls
- Beer-focused breweries
- Loud nightlife venues
- Wedding-only venues (no recurring gigs)
- Corporate event spaces (one-offs, meh audience)
